blocks.text: You are a senior code reviewer. Review the ZCrystal Plugin codebase at /home/snow/.openclaw/workspace/zcrystal-plugin/src/. Focus on: 1) Architectural issues and redundant modules 2) Code quality problems (error handling, type safety) 3) Potential bugs or security issues 4) Performance concerns 5) Suggestions for cleanup. Key files: index.ts, honcho-client.ts, tools/ directory (core-tools.ts, skill-tools.ts, workflow-tools.ts, system-tools.ts, proactive-tools.ts), self-evolution.ts, skill-manager.ts. Provide a concise JSON report with: critical_issues (array of {severity, file, issue, fix}), improvements (array of {file, suggestion}), working_well (array of {file, what}), priority_fixes (array of {priority, description}). Output as valid JSON only, no markdown formatting.

blocks.text: {"critical_issues":[{"severity":"critical","file":"src/index.ts (registerCoreTools call, lines ~61-81)","issue":"core-tools.ts calls skillManager.getSkills().ok/.data, getSkill().ok/.data, and readContent() which don't exist on the local SkillManager class. The local SkillManager (skill-manager.ts) returns Skill[] directly and has no Result wrapper. @zcrystal/evo's createSkillManager returns Promise<Result<...>> with .ok/.data. This causes runtime 'undefined is not a function' errors.","fix":"Either (a) replace local skill-manager.ts with a thin wrapper around @zcrystal/evo's createSkillManager, or (b) update core-tools.ts to use the direct-return API (remove .ok/.data checks). The local SkillManager's discover() returns Skill[] synchronously; getSkill() returns Skill|undefined directly; readSkillContent() returns Promise<string> not Result<>. Choose one pattern consistently."},{"severity":"critical","file":"src/honcho-client.ts + src/honcho-trace.ts","issue":"Duplicate trace storage: HonchoClient.learnFromUser/getTraces and HonchoTraceStore both write to 'zcrystal-traces' session with INCOMPATIBLE JSON schemas. HonchoClient uses {type,skill,action,success,details,timestamp} while HonchoTraceStore uses {type,skill,action,success,details,timestamp}. Both store to same session and read from same getMessages() output - parsing will fail silently when one writes a trace the other reads. Additionally HonchoClient.getTraces() has untyped return (unknown[]).","fix":"Unify trace storage into a single class. Standardize the JSON schema (consider a shared TraceSchema interface in types.ts). Make getTraces() return a properly typed Trace[]."},{"severity":"critical","file":"src/index.ts (fts5Search/fts5Stats, lines 104-143)","issue":"fts5Search and fts5Stats ignore the {success, data/error} result and blindly cast data as string. When FTS5 MCP server is down or returns unexpected JSON, okResult gets String(an error object) instead of actual search results. No validation of response.json() structure beyond existence of result.content[0].text.","fix":"Validate result.success before extracting data. Add JSON schema validation for the response structure. Return errResult with descriptive error when FTS5 is unreachable. Add retry logic or graceful degradation."},{"severity":"critical","file":"src/self-evolution.ts (performRollback lines 591-610, commitEvolution lines 358-374)","issue":"performRollback is called internally by verifyAppliedCandidate but its async fire-and-forget IIFE (backup creation) creates content AFTER the rollback function has already returned. The rollback writes skill.content from this.backups, but the backups map may not be populated yet since the write is non-awaited. Race condition: rollback could execute before backup is saved, corrupting skill content.","fix":"Remove the async IIFE in performRollback. Create the backup synchronously BEFORE writeSkillContent is called (make it async and await it). Similarly fix commitEvolution's inline backup creation which is also fire-and-forget."},{"severity":"critical","file":"src/index.ts (registerHook message:received, lines ~587-600)","issue":"api.registerHook('message:received') casts event to {context?: {content?: string}} without input validation. If Honcho sends a non-JSON event or the event structure differs, (event as...).context.content access throws. Also 'message:sent' hook accesses (event as {content?: string}).content directly without nested context check.","fix":"Add input validation: if (typeof event !== 'object' || event === null) return early. Use optional chaining: (event as any)?.context?.content ?? ''. Log unknown event shapes for debugging. Add a schema validator for incoming hook events."}],"improvements":[{"file":"src/index.ts","suggestion":"PluginState uses ReturnType<typeof createHonchoClient> and ReturnType<typeof createSkillManager> which loses type specificity. Define concrete interfaces for HonchoClient and SkillManager (or re-export from their source modules) to get proper autocomplete and type safety. Currently 20+ state fields are typed as 'any' effectively."},{"file":"src/index.ts","suggestion":"Duplicate evolution scheduling: EvolutionScheduler is instantiated in index.ts (lines 213-221) and a separate one may be created inside UnifiedApiRouter. Two independent schedulers could trigger double evolution. Check if @zcrystal/evo's UnifiedApiRouter already starts its own scheduler - if so, remove the manual one or document the dual-scheduler design with a clear comment explaining why both are needed."},{"file":"src/index.ts","suggestion":"Hardcoded tool count '95' in console.log is a maintenance hazard. Compute dynamically: const toolCount = count registered tools, or remove the hardcoded number entirely."},{"file":"src/index.ts","suggestion":"zcrystal_toolhub_logs calls state.toolHub.getLogs(undefined, ...) - first arg should be a log level or null, but passing undefined is misleading. Document the expected parameter type or use a named parameter."},{"file":"src/honcho-client.ts","suggestion":"Inconsistent error handling: most methods silently catch exceptions and return empty/false with no logging. addMessages, peer, session, listSessions all swallow errors. Consider adding a debug-mode logger so failures are at least visible during development. At minimum, log at 'warn' level for repeated failures."},{"file":"src/honcho-client.ts","suggestion":"search() returns SearchResult[] but getMessages() returns Message[] - the search endpoint response type doesn't match the declared SearchResult[] type (it's actually the raw array from the API). Add explicit parsing to your SearchResult interface."},{"file":"src/self-evolution.ts","suggestion":"persistTrace() and loadRecoveryPoints() are stub placeholders with no actual disk I/O. This breaks the 'Memory Persistence - Dual Storage' pattern (Pattern 1) documented in the file header. Either implement actual JSON file persistence or remove the pattern documentation claim. The same applies to skill content backup - no actual disk writes occur."},{"file":"src/self-evolution.ts","suggestion":"Auto-trigger evolution in checkAutoTrigger() fires evolveSkill() as fire-and-forget without any coordination. If multiple skills trigger simultaneously (each with low success rate), all will run Promise.all races in evolveAllSkills(). Consider adding a concurrency limit or mutex to prevent resource exhaustion."},{"file":"src/self-evolution.ts","suggestion":"getHistory() returns a copy of the array ([...this.evolutionHistory]) but getLastEvolution() returns the actual array element (no copy). For consistency and to prevent accidental mutation, return a copy from getLastEvolution too: return [...this.evolutionHistory].pop()."},{"file":"src/skill-manager.ts","suggestion":"writeSkillContent() uses a dynamic import for writeFile inside the function body: const { writeFile } = await import('node:fs/promises'). This should be a static import at the top of the file. Dynamic imports inside async functions add overhead and make tree-shaking less effective."},{"file":"src/skill-manager.ts","suggestion":"parseSkillContent() regex-based YAML parsing is fragile. Frontmatter metadata JSON block parsing with regex (metaMatch) could fail on valid YAML. Consider using a lightweight YAML parser (e.g., yaml npm package) for production-quality frontmatter parsing."},{"file":"src/skill-manager.ts","suggestion":"MAX_WRITES_PER_DAY is 1000 but honcho-client.ts uses a local _writeCountToday counter that is reset in memory only. On plugin restart, the count resets. This means the 1000/day limit is per-session not per-day as documented. If the plugin restarts, unlimited writes become possible.","fix":"Persist _writeCountToday and _writeCountDate to disk (e.g., a JSON file in config.paths.temp) so the daily limit survives restarts."},{"file":"src/tools/system-tools.ts","suggestion":"zcrystal_hook_register always returns errResult explaining it can't work via a tool call, yet it IS registered as a tool. This is misleading to agents. Either remove it, implement actual registration via a different mechanism, or return okResult with a clear message explaining the correct registration method."},{"file":"src/tools/system-tools.ts","suggestion":"zcrystal_indexer_rebuild returns 'not yet implemented' error - stub tool exposed to agents. Either implement it or document that it's intentionally placeholder and won't be called."},{"file":"src/tools/proactive-tools.ts","suggestion":"zcrystal_layers_exchange returns a static placeholder string - no actual layer exchange is performed. This provides no value to callers. Implement actual layer compression/promotion logic or remove the tool."},{"file":"src/tools/proactive-tools.ts","suggestion":"zcrystal_correction_list hardcodes correctionKeys = ['correction:latest', 'correction:history'] and iterates over them. But zcrystal_correction_add stores data as 'correction:{timestamp}'. The list function will never find any corrections because keys don't match the pattern used by add. This is a silent data loss bug."},{"file":"src/tools/index.ts","suggestion":"File is a simple re-export with no added value. Since index.ts imports directly from './tools/*.js', this barrel file adds an unnecessary indirection. Either use it consistently throughout (import from './tools/index.js') or remove it entirely to reduce indirection."}],"working_well":[{"file":"src/honcho-client.ts","what":"Comprehensive safety gates: daily write count + content deduplication hash effectively prevent feedback loops and runaway writes. The _checkAndRecordWrite() logic is well-designed with clear console warnings at each gate."},{"file":"src/honcho-client.ts","what":"Well-structured Honcho API alignment with detailed comments referencing the official API. Auth header injection pattern is clean. ensureWorkspace() follows proper create-or-get semantics."},{"file":"src/self-evolution.ts","what":"Closed-loop evolution with verification tracking (appliedCandidates, VERIFICATION_COUNT, DEGRADATION_THRESHOLD) is a sophisticated design. The rollback mechanism with explicit backup storage before mutation is architecturally sound."},{"file":"src/self-evolution.ts","what":"13 Harness Patterns documentation in the file header provides clear architectural context. Typed IDs (EvolutionId, TraceId using template literal types) enforce compile-time correctness for task decomposition."},{"file":"src/self-evolution.ts","what":"Promise memoization (pendingEvaluations Map) prevents duplicate LLM evaluation requests for the same candidate. This is an effective performance optimization."},{"file":"src/self-evolution.ts","what":"Robust JSON parsing fallback chain: tries direct parse first, then regex extraction from markdown, then falls back to rule-based evaluation. Prevents single parse failure from breaking evolution."},{"file":"src/skill-manager.ts","what":"Recursive directory discovery with configurable depth limit (depth > 3) prevents infinite recursion. Graceful handling of missing directories and inaccessible paths."},{"file":"src/skill-manager.ts","what":"SKILL.md discovery pattern matches standard OpenClaw skill conventions. Frontmatter parsing extracts name/slug/version/description from standard YAML frontmatter."},{"file":"src/tools/","what":"Consistent tool registration pattern across all tool files using TypeScript TypeBox for parameter schema validation. Each domain (core, task, skill, workflow, system, proactive) is cleanly separated into its own file with a clear index re-export."},{"file":"src/index.ts","what":"Plugin state management with a single nullable state variable and explicit null checks before all state access is a clean pattern. The okResult/errResult factory functions provide consistent response formatting across all 95+ tools."},{"file":"src/index.ts","what":"Heartbeat and proactive check intervals run as separate setInterval loops with proper error wrapping, preventing one failure from killing the maintenance system."}],"priority_fixes":[{"priority":1,"description":"Fix SkillManager API mismatch in core-tools.ts: the local SkillManager doesn't have .ok/.data Result wrappers. Either swap to @zcrystal/evo's typed version or remove .ok/.data checks from all tool calls to skillManager."},{"priority":2,"description":"Fix fire-and-forget async IIFE in self-evolution.ts performRollback/commitEvolution. The backup creation must be awaited BEFORE the rollback write executes, not after."},{"priority":3,"description":"Unify HonchoClient trace storage with HonchoTraceStore into a single schema. The current duplicate write paths with incompatible JSON schemas in the same 'zcrystal-traces' session will cause silent parse failures."},{"priority":4,"description":"Fix zcrystal_correction_list key mismatch: stores corrections as 'correction:{timestamp}' but list searches for 'correction:latest' and 'correction:history'. List will always return empty."},{"priority":5,"description":"Validate FTS5 response structure before returning data to agents. Add proper JSON schema checking so a failed FTS5 server doesn't return garbage as search results."},{"priority":6,"description":"Persist honcho-client.ts daily write count to disk so the 1000/day limit survives plugin restarts (currently per-session only)."},{"priority":7,"description":"Add input validation to message:received and message:sent hooks to prevent runtime exceptions on unexpected event shapes."}]}
